app.name: TUI Test
app.one_liner: An end-to-end terminal testing framework for CLI and TUI experiences that provides fast, reliable, and isolated tests across macOS, Linux, and Windows with support for a wide range of shells.

screens.name: Test Runner Interface
screens.purpose: Execute and manage terminal-based end-to-end tests with real-time feedback and debugging capabilities.

rust_commands.name: capture_snapshot
rust_commands.purpose: Generate a detailed terminal state snapshot for assertion or debugging purposes.

rust_commands.name: run_test
rust_commands.purpose: Launch an isolated terminal context, execute a sequence of interactions, and return test results including exit code and captured output.

rust_commands.name: wait_for_render
rust_commands.purpose: Pause execution until the terminal has rendered expected content, leveraging auto-wait logic.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add auto-wait mechanism for terminal readiness and render cycles
week 1: Enable snapshot capture and comparison
week 1: Implement core terminal context creation with xterm.js headless mode
week 1: Support basic interaction commands (type, enter, wait)
acceptance_tests: A test can launch a bash terminal on Linux, run 'echo hello', and assert that 'hello' appears in the output.
Each test runs in full isolation with its own PTY and terminal emulator instance, confirmed by parallel execution without interference.
Terminal snapshots include full state (cursor position, colors, buffer) and can be serialized for debugging.
The framework automatically waits for the terminal to be ready before sending input, eliminating manual sleep calls.
